data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — NO G4 cross-child finding; empty findings list.

7-mode walk:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 2359 co-edits batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py with 33d0, but declares depends_on 33d0 ("the counts come from one path"); 33d0 collapsed to one production path first, then 2359 instrumented the single survivor. Sequenced, not concurrent.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. The earlier-flagged labels_applied list-vs-int conflict is resolved in the as-shipped code: 33d0's collapse deleted the old list-valued payload key, and 2359 mints all per-sub-op counts (labels/comments/links) fresh as integers, so the computed>0 && applied==0 canary is well-typed over all three kinds. 2359's cited int-valued inbound links_applied parity model is now accurate for every kind.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. Only input dep (story D/33d0) is correctly declared; needs no schema/fixture input from 8937/fe57.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 2359 (canary), 33d0 (combined multi-sub-op UPDATE), 822a (bidirectional round-trip) are distinct intents over the same production path; consolidation→33d0 and probe wiring→822a declared OUT consistently. 822a asserts round-trip outcomes, not the *_applied telemetry payload.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 33d0→2359 ordering captured by depends_on; collapse-then-instrument is correct.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. Added outcome keys + RECON sub-op fields are purely additive; AC5 asserts the count invariant, not log strings. No brittle downstream expectation imposed.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Additive plumbing on the surviving batch path; references no part of the deleted typed leaf; cited parity is with intact apply_inbound links_applied.

The parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause), not a G4 cross-child inconsistency.

Findings list is EMPTY for this pairing.
